title: AI-901 Mock Exam 2: Applied Workloads and Responsible AI
description: Challenging unofficial AI-901 practice focused on scenario selection, lightweight apps, text, speech, vision, agents, and operational controls. Based on the public Microsoft AI-901 skills guide, Microsoft Foundry, Agent Service, and Content Understanding documentation. Original practice questions; not official exam content.

items.prompt: A developer creates an AI app that must search enterprise documents, call an inventory API, and decide whether to create a purchase request. Which workload pattern best describes this solution?
options: Agentic AI
Ground the response with trusted data and validate outputs before use
Use a prompt agent with configured instructions and tools
Use a hosted agent when custom orchestration code is required

items.prompt: A model answers general questions well but hallucinates current company policy. What should the developer add?
items.answer: Grounding with an approved policy data source
options: Grounding with an approved policy data source
Azure AI Search vector indexing without field extraction
A multimodal model prompt without a defined extraction schema
Document Intelligence-style OCR followed by manual review

items.prompt: A legal review assistant must cite only the paragraphs it used from uploaded contracts. Which tool is most relevant?
options: File search with grounded retrieval
Web search
Memory
A custom function or API tool with controlled authentication

items.prompt: A support agent must open tickets through an internal REST API. Which tool type is most appropriate?
items.answer: A custom function or API tool with controlled authentication
options: A custom function or API tool with controlled authentication
Memory
A connected MCP server with scoped tools
File search

items.prompt: A developer wants Foundry to run a portal-authored agent with no custom compute to manage. Which option should they choose?
options: Prompt agent
Use a lightweight client that invokes a published agent endpoint
Hosted agent
Call the Responses API directly from the existing process

items.prompt: A developer packages custom orchestration as a container and wants Foundry to host it with managed identity and observability. Which option should they choose?
options: Hosted agent
Prompt agent
Call the Responses API directly from the existing process
Create a custom tool hosted outside Foundry and call it from a prompt agent

items.prompt: A user asks: "Summarize this contract and identify renewal dates." The app passes a PDF directly to a document extraction workflow before summarization. Which service area is involved?
options: Content Understanding for documents
Azure AI Search vector indexing without field extraction
A multimodal model prompt without a defined extraction schema
Document Intelligence-style OCR followed by manual review

items.prompt: A project needs to process forms, images, audio recordings, and videos with structured outputs. Which Foundry Tools capability is designed for this?
options: Azure Content Understanding
A multimodal model prompt without a defined extraction schema
Document Intelligence-style OCR followed by manual review
Content Understanding with confidence scores and grounding enabled

items.prompt: A developer must create a lightweight app using Foundry SDKs. Which prerequisite is most relevant according to the exam scope?
items.answer: Familiarity with Azure resources and Python syntax or programming techniques
options: Familiarity with Azure resources and Python syntax or programming techniques
Use Content Understanding when structured extraction from multimodal content is required
Use tracing and evaluations to diagnose model and tool behavior
Ground the response with trusted data and validate outputs before use

items.prompt: A REST call to a Foundry endpoint fails because the token audience/identity is wrong. Which knowledge area is being tested?
items.answer: Foundational technical skills for Azure resources and authentication
options: Foundational technical skills for Azure resources and authentication
Language detection
Entity detection
Key phrase extraction

items.prompt: A developer wants a chat client to call a deployed model in Foundry. Which API pattern is most aligned with current Foundry docs?
items.answer: Use the project endpoint and Responses API through an SDK or REST
options: Use the project endpoint and Responses API through an SDK or REST
Prompt agent
Hosted agent
Call the Responses API directly from the existing process

items.prompt: A team is deciding whether to use a multimodal model. Which requirement most strongly indicates that they need one?
items.answer: The prompt includes both text and images
options: The prompt includes both text and images
A text-only chat model with no visual input
Content Understanding image extraction
Image generation from a text prompt

items.prompt: A voice assistant receives spoken prompts and answers using a deployed multimodal model. Which input processing is still commonly required?
items.answer: Speech audio must be captured and sent in a supported format or transcribed depending on the model/API
options: Speech audio must be captured and sent in a supported format or transcribed depending on the model/API
Text analysis over manually typed transcripts only
A multimodal image model with no audio handling
Speech translation when source and target languages differ

items.prompt: An app must translate spoken phrases from one language to another during a call. Which capability is most relevant?
options: Speech translation
A multimodal image model with no audio handling
Speech translation when source and target languages differ
Speech recognition followed by model processing and speech synthesis

items.prompt: A retail app needs to locate each product on a shelf image. Which computer vision task best fits?
options: Object detection
OCR or visual text extraction
A deployed multimodal model that accepts image input
A text-only chat model with no visual input

items.prompt: A mobile app must identify whether an uploaded image is a receipt, invoice, or business card. Which vision task best fits?
options: Image classification
A deployed multimodal model that accepts image input
A text-only chat model with no visual input
Content Understanding image extraction

items.prompt: A vision app needs to describe a photo for a user who cannot see it. Which capability is most relevant?
options: Image captioning or visual description
A text-only chat model with no visual input
Content Understanding image extraction
Image generation from a text prompt

items.prompt: A prompt asks a model to create an infographic from a text description. Which workload is involved?
options: Image generation
Content Understanding image extraction
Image generation from a text prompt
OCR or visual text extraction

items.prompt: A model is asked to rewrite customer emails in a friendlier tone. Which workload is primarily involved?
options: Generative AI for text transformation
Change the model deployment and re-test quality, latency, and cost
Use grounding instead of relying only on model pretraining
Lower temperature and constrain maximum output tokens

items.prompt: A system extracts "Seattle", "Microsoft", and "July 1" from a news paragraph. Which technique is this?
options: Named entity recognition
Language detection
Entity detection
Key phrase extraction

items.prompt: A system reports the key terms "refund delay", "shipping damage", and "missing invoice" from support cases. Which technique is this?
options: Key phrase extraction
Entity detection
Sentiment analysis
Summarization

items.prompt: A model outputs "negative" for a customer review. Which technique is this?
options: Sentiment analysis
Key phrase extraction
Summarization
Language detection

items.prompt: A system condenses a call transcript into decisions, risks, and action items. Which text technique is central?
options: Summarization
Sentiment analysis
Language detection
Entity detection

items.prompt: A team wants users to know when they are interacting with AI and what the AI can and cannot do. Which responsible AI principle is most relevant?
options: Transparency
Accountability
Fairness
Reliability and safety

items.prompt: A model is evaluated against groups of users to ensure comparable error rates. Which responsible AI principle is most relevant?
options: Fairness
Reliability and safety
Privacy and security
Inclusiveness

items.prompt: A service stores prompts containing personal data. Which controls are most relevant?
items.answer: Data minimization, access control, retention policy, and redaction
options: Data minimization, access control, retention policy, and redaction
Ground the response with trusted data and validate outputs before use
Use a prompt agent with configured instructions and tools
Use a hosted agent when custom orchestration code is required

items.prompt: An assistant supports only one language and cannot be used by a large portion of intended users. Which principle is most affected?
options: Inclusiveness
Privacy and security
Transparency
Accountability

items.prompt: A team documents who approves prompts, monitors failures, and owns incident response. Which principle is addressed?
options: Accountability
Inclusiveness
Transparency
Fairness

items.prompt: A developer wants to reduce harmful generated content. Which should be configured?
items.answer: Content filters, safety instructions, and evaluation
options: Content filters, safety instructions, and evaluation
Use Content Understanding when structured extraction from multimodal content is required
Use tracing and evaluations to diagnose model and tool behavior
Ground the response with trusted data and validate outputs before use

items.prompt: An agent decides between web search and file search. What determines the appropriate tool choice?
items.answer: The user goal, instructions, available tools, and grounding requirements
options: The user goal, instructions, available tools, and grounding requirements
A connected MCP server with scoped tools
File search
Code interpreter

items.prompt: A developer needs to inspect every decision an agent made during a run. Which feature is most helpful?
options: End-to-end tracing
Ground the response with trusted data and validate outputs before use
Use a prompt agent with configured instructions and tools
Use a hosted agent when custom orchestration code is required

items.prompt: A team wants to expose a stable endpoint for an approved agent while developers keep testing changes. What should they use?
options: Published agent versions
Use a prompt agent with configured instructions and tools
Use a hosted agent when custom orchestration code is required
Use Content Understanding when structured extraction from multimodal content is required

items.prompt: An agent must call tools on behalf of a signed-in user. Which authentication concept is most relevant?
items.answer: On-Behalf-Of or delegated authentication for tools
options: On-Behalf-Of or delegated authentication for tools
Use a hosted agent when custom orchestration code is required
Use Content Understanding when structured extraction from multimodal content is required
Use tracing and evaluations to diagnose model and tool behavior

items.prompt: A project must govern which users can deploy models and edit agents. Which Azure feature is most relevant?
options: RBAC
Change the model deployment and re-test quality, latency, and cost
Use grounding instead of relying only on model pretraining
Lower temperature and constrain maximum output tokens

items.prompt: A model produces a correct answer but includes extra text that breaks JSON parsing. What is the best mitigation?
items.answer: Constrain the response format and validate output before parsing
options: Constrain the response format and validate output before parsing
Use grounding instead of relying only on model pretraining
Lower temperature and constrain maximum output tokens
Use a smaller low-latency model after evaluation

items.prompt: A developer needs current product prices in answers. Which approach is most appropriate?
items.answer: Connect a trusted current data source through retrieval or a tool
options: Connect a trusted current data source through retrieval or a tool
Ground the response with trusted data and validate outputs before use
Use a prompt agent with configured instructions and tools
Use a hosted agent when custom orchestration code is required

items.prompt: An app must not take irreversible actions without human approval. What design should it use?
items.answer: Require confirmation or human-in-the-loop controls before tool execution
options: Require confirmation or human-in-the-loop controls before tool execution
Use a prompt agent with configured instructions and tools
Use a hosted agent when custom orchestration code is required
Use Content Understanding when structured extraction from multimodal content is required

items.prompt: A support assistant should answer in the company tone, refuse unsafe requests, and ask for missing account IDs. Where should these persistent rules go?
options: System instructions
Use a hosted agent when custom orchestration code is required
Use Content Understanding when structured extraction from multimodal content is required
Use tracing and evaluations to diagnose model and tool behavior

items.prompt: A developer supplies three examples of correctly classified tickets before asking the model to classify a new ticket. What technique is used?
options: Few-shot prompting
Change the model deployment and re-test quality, latency, and cost
Use grounding instead of relying only on model pretraining
Lower temperature and constrain maximum output tokens

items.prompt: A developer wants a one-call model interaction that can use tools and return a structured response. Which current Foundry-aligned API concept is most relevant?
options: Responses API
Use a lightweight client that invokes a published agent endpoint
Prompt agent
Hosted agent

items.prompt: A Foundry project must support Python and TypeScript developers. What does Foundry provide?
items.answer: SDKs and REST APIs for multiple languages
options: SDKs and REST APIs for multiple languages
Ground the response with trusted data and validate outputs before use
Use a prompt agent with configured instructions and tools
Use a hosted agent when custom orchestration code is required

items.prompt: A team asks whether preview capabilities can appear on the exam. What is the most accurate expectation?
items.answer: Most questions cover GA features, but commonly used preview features may appear
options: Most questions cover GA features, but commonly used preview features may appear
Use a prompt agent with configured instructions and tools
Use a hosted agent when custom orchestration code is required
Use Content Understanding when structured extraction from multimodal content is required

items.prompt: A lightweight chat app returns 401 errors from Foundry. The endpoint is correct. What should be checked next?
items.answer: Credential source, token permissions, and project RBAC
options: Credential source, token permissions, and project RBAC
A managed identity or DefaultAzureCredential-based app identity
Anonymous access with tool permissions disabled
OAuth On-Behalf-Of for user-delegated tool access

items.prompt: A developer wants to compare answer quality before selecting a model for production. What should they do?
items.answer: Evaluate candidate models against representative prompts and quality criteria
options: Evaluate candidate models against representative prompts and quality criteria
Change the model deployment and re-test quality, latency, and cost
Use grounding instead of relying only on model pretraining
Lower temperature and constrain maximum output tokens

items.prompt: A service needs low latency and can tolerate less reasoning depth. Which model family characteristic is preferred?
items.answer: Smaller or mini model optimized for speed
options: Smaller or mini model optimized for speed
Use grounding instead of relying only on model pretraining
Lower temperature and constrain maximum output tokens
Use a smaller low-latency model after evaluation

items.prompt: A user asks for a medical diagnosis. Which behavior best supports responsible AI for a general assistant?
items.answer: Provide limitations and encourage professional medical advice rather than making unsupported claims
options: Provide limitations and encourage professional medical advice rather than making unsupported claims
Ground the response with trusted data and validate outputs before use
Use a prompt agent with configured instructions and tools
Use a hosted agent when custom orchestration code is required

items.prompt: A moderation workflow stores all public uploads as pending until reviewed. Which dataset status should be shown in the public gallery?
options: Only approved datasets
Use a prompt agent with configured instructions and tools
Use a hosted agent when custom orchestration code is required
Use Content Understanding when structured extraction from multimodal content is required

items.prompt: A model is connected to tools that can access confidential files. What is the most important principle?
items.answer: Least privilege for identities and tool access
options: Least privilege for identities and tool access
Use a larger reasoning model for complex multi-step tasks
Change the model deployment and re-test quality, latency, and cost
Use grounding instead of relying only on model pretraining

items.prompt: An app must extract policy numbers from scanned images and validate them against a database. Which implementation pattern is best?
items.answer: Use image/document extraction, validate extracted values, then call the database tool or API
options: Use image/document extraction, validate extracted values, then call the database tool or API
Image generation from a text prompt
OCR or visual text extraction
A deployed multimodal model that accepts image input

items.prompt: A team wants the assistant to remember context during a single multi-turn conversation but not use it for unrelated users. Which design concern is involved?
options: Conversation state and memory scope
Code interpreter
Web search
Memory
